question: Simplify fully $5xy^2 \times (5x^2y)^{-3} \times 5x^2y$, where $x$ and $y$ are positive.

Air is trapped in a cylinder by a piston. The density of the trapped air is $\rho$. The piston is moved so that the pressure of the trapped air increases by 20%. The temperature of the trapped air does not change. What is the new density of the trapped air? (Assume that air is an ideal gas.)

steps.title: For an ideal gas at constant temperature so
steps.content: For an ideal gas at constant temperature, $PV=nRT$ so $P \propto 1/V \propto \rho$ (since $\rho=m/V$ and mass is fixed).

steps.title: A increase in P gives a increase in
steps.content: A 20% increase in P gives a 20% increase in density: $\rho_{new}=1.20\rho$.

subtopic: Changing the subject of a formula
question: Which of the following is a rearrangement of $\dfrac{p}{2} + \dfrac{3}{q} = \dfrac{4}{r}$ so that $q$ is the subject?

A non-ideal transformer has 100 turns on the primary coil and 25 turns on the secondary coil. It is provided with 3.0 kW of electrical power at a current of 12.5 A. The voltage output is the same as for an ideal transformer, but the current in the output coil is 40 A. What is the efficiency of the transformer?

Two solid cylinders, P and Q, are shown, where $x>y$. Cylinder P has diameter $x$ and height $y$. Cylinder Q has diameter $y$ and height $x$. What is the positive difference between the total surface areas of P and Q?

subtopic: Elastic potential energy in a spring
question: A light spring has an uncompressed length of 0.10 m. When an object of mass 0.5 kg rests in equilibrium on top of the spring, the length of the spring reduces to 0.08 m. What is the energy stored in the spring due to the compression? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; the spring obeys Hooke's law)

The price of item P is reduced by 10%. The next day, the new price is increased by 10%. The price of item Q is increased by 10%. The next day, the new price is reduced by 10%. How does the final price of each item compare to the original price of that item?

options.A: P: lower than original; Q: lower than original
options.B: P: lower than original; Q: higher than original
options.C: P: higher than original; Q: lower than original
options.D: P: higher than original; Q: higher than original
options.E: P: the same as original; Q: the same as original

steps.title: Both end up lower than original regardless of
steps.content: Both end up 1% lower than original, regardless of order.

subtopic: Series circuit with a short-circuited component
question: A set of decorative lights consists of 20 lamps connected in series to a dc supply of constant voltage. The total power transferred by all the lamps is P. The set is designed so that if one of the lamps fails, that lamp becomes short-circuited (zero resistance); the remaining lamps stay lit. If this happens, with the set connected to the same supply, what is the new total power transferred by the remaining 19 lamps? (Assume the resistance of each functioning lamp remains constant.)

steps.title: Let each lamp have resistance and supply voltage
steps.content: Let each lamp have resistance $r$ and supply voltage $V$.

steps.title: After one lamp shorts total resistance is so
steps.content: After one lamp shorts, total resistance is $19r$, so new power $=V^2/(19r) = \left(\frac{20}{19}\right)P$.

question: [DIAGRAM: Triangle SQT is right-angled at Q, with point R on SQ such that SR:RQ = 1:3. Triangle QRP is right-angled at Q. PR = ST = 8 cm, QT = 4 cm.] What is the length of PQ, in cm?

steps.title: In right triangle SQT right angle at Q
steps.content: In right triangle SQT (right angle at Q): $ST=8$ (hypotenuse), $QT=4$, so $SQ=\sqrt{8^2-4^2}=\sqrt{48}=4\sqrt{3}$.

steps.title: In right triangle QRP right angle at Q
steps.content: In right triangle QRP (right angle at Q): $PR=8$ (hypotenuse), $RQ=3\sqrt{3}$, so $PQ=\sqrt{64-27}=\sqrt{37}$.

subtopic: Momentum-time graph and Newton's second law
question: [DIAGRAM: graph of momentum (×10^6 kg m s^-1) vs time (s) for an accelerating train, a straight line rising from the origin to about 12×10^6 kg m s^-1 at t = 4 s] A train accelerates from rest along a straight, horizontal track. The force exerted by its motors is constant and there is a constant friction force of $1.8\times10^7$ N. The graph shows how the momentum of the train changes with time. What is the force exerted on the train by its motors?

steps.title: Net force rate of change of momentum gradient
steps.content: Net force = rate of change of momentum = gradient of graph $\approx (12\times10^6)/4 = 3.0\times10^6$ N.

steps.title: Net force motor force friction so motor force
steps.content: Net force = motor force $-$ friction, so motor force $=3.0\times10^6+1.8\times10^7=2.1\times10^7$ N.

question: The curve with equation $y=x^2-4x+5$ meets the straight line with equation $y=2x+c$ at two points, which have x-coordinates $p$ and $q$, where $q>p$. Given that $q-p=8$, what is the value of the constant $c$?

A ship travels into a wave that is travelling in the opposite direction to the ship. The ship has a horizontal speed of 8.0 m s$^{-1}$. The speed of the wave is 3.0 m s$^{-1}$. The front of the ship rises and falls with a time period of 8.0 s. What is the wavelength of the wave?

steps.title: Since ship and wave move toward each other
steps.content: Since ship and wave move toward each other, the relative (encounter) speed is $8.0+3.0=11$ m/s.

steps.title: The s period observed is the encounter period
steps.content: The 8.0 s period observed is the encounter period, so $\lambda = v_{rel}\times T = 11\times8=88$ m.

question: Given that $y=\dfrac{\sin60°-1}{\cos60°}$, what is the value of $y^3$?

subtopic: V-I characteristic of a filament lamp
question: [DIAGRAM: circuit with a 6.0 V battery connected to an 8.0 Ω resistor in series with a filament lamp and an ammeter] The reading on the ammeter is 0.25 A. Which graph is a possible V-I graph for the filament lamp? (Graphs A-F show V (up to 2.0, 4.0, or 6.0 V) plotted against I (0 to 0.25 A), with curves of varying shape.)

steps.title: Voltage across the resistor V
steps.content: Voltage across the 8.0 Ω resistor $=0.25\times8=2.0$ V.

steps.title: The correct graph must pass through with a
steps.content: The correct graph must pass through $(one,V)=(0.25\text{ A}, 4.0\text{ V})$ with a curve typical of a filament lamp (resistance increasing with current).

question: Charlie has a bowl containing red sweets and green sweets only. The sweets are identical in all respects except colour. There are nine sweets in total in the bowl. Charlie eats two sweets from the bowl at random. The probability of Charlie not eating any green sweets is $\frac{5}{12}$. What is the probability that Charlie eats two green sweets?

steps.title: Let number of red sweets so
steps.content: Let $r$ = number of red sweets. $P(\text{no green})=\binom{r}{2}/\binom{9}{2}=5/12$. $\binom{9}{2}=36$, so $\binom{r}{2}=15 \Rightarrow r(r-1)=30 \Rightarrow r=6$.

question: A radioactive nuclide X decays in a single stage to a stable nuclide R. A radioactive nuclide Y decays in a single stage to a stable nuclide S. When a rock formed it contained equal numbers of atoms of all four nuclides X, Y, R and S. The half-life of X is $T$ years and the half-life of Y is $2T$ years. What is the value of $\dfrac{\text{number of atoms of S}}{\text{number of atoms of R}}$ at a time $4T$ years after the rock formed? (Assume no other processes add or remove X, Y, R or S from the rock.)

The greatest diagonal distance between two vertices of a cuboid is $\sqrt{77}$ cm. A similar cuboid has all its lengths exactly half the lengths of the original cuboid. The sides of this smaller cuboid are 2 cm, 3 cm and $x$ cm. What is the value of $x$, in cm?

steps.title: Original cuboid has dimensions double the small one
steps.content: Original cuboid has dimensions double the small one: $4,\,6,\,2x$.

subtopic: Latent heat and specific heat capacity
question: A beaker containing 180 g of water at 25 °C has a 20 g ice cube at 0 °C added to it. No heat is transferred between the water and the surroundings (including the beaker). What is the final temperature of all the water in the beaker after all the ice has melted? (Specific heat capacity of water = 4 J g$^{-1}$ °C$^{-1}$; specific latent heat of fusion of water = 300 J g$^{-1}$.)

steps.title: Heat lost by warm water heat to melt
steps.content: Heat lost by warm water = heat to melt ice + heat to warm melted ice to $T$: $180\times4\times(25-T)=20\times300+20\times4\times T$.

question: A car journey is $m$ miles long. One kilometre is equivalent to $x$ miles. The car uses one litre of fuel to travel a distance of $f$ kilometres. Fuel for the car costs $p$ pence per litre. Which of the following expressions gives the cost of fuel for this journey, in pounds? (There are 100 pence in one pound.)

A pulse of ultrasound travels from one end of a solid uniform rod of length $L$, starting at time $t=0$. The pulse is partially reflected by a crack in the rod and partially by the far end of the rod. These two reflected pulses travel back along the rod, arriving at the end from which they started at times $t_1$ and $t_2$, where $t_2>t_1$. What is the distance between the crack and the far end of the rod?

steps.title: Speed of sound in rod from the far
steps.content: Speed of sound in rod $v=2L/t_2$ (from the far-end echo).

steps.title: Distance to crack
steps.content: Distance to crack $d=v t_1/2 = L t_1/t_2$.

steps.title: Distance from crack to far end
steps.content: Distance from crack to far end $=L-d=L\left(1-\frac{t_1}{t_2}\right)=\frac{(t_2-t_1)}{t_2}L$.

question: Given that $y=\left(2\sqrt{x}-\dfrac{1}{2\sqrt{x}}\right)^2$, find the value of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ when $x=\dfrac12$.

steps.title: Evaluate dy/dx at x equals one half

subtopic: Newton's second law on inclined and horizontal planes
question: Object P of mass 2.4 kg is on a smooth plane inclined at 60° to the horizontal. A constant force of magnitude $2F$ parallel to the plane is applied to P, so P moves directly up the plane with constant velocity. Object Q of mass 0.75 kg is on a smooth, horizontal plane. A constant force of magnitude $F$ parallel to the plane is applied to Q, so Q moves with constant acceleration. What is the acceleration of Q? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)

steps.title: For P constant velocity N so N
steps.content: For P (constant velocity): $2F=mg\sin60^\circ=2.4\times10\times\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}=12\sqrt{3}$ N, so $F=6\sqrt{3}$ N.

question: A particular arithmetic series has first term $a$ and common difference $d$. The sum of the first $k$ terms of this series is denoted $S_k$. Which of the following is a simplification of $S_{n+1}-S_{n-1}$?

subtopic: Sound wave frequency and displacement from a waveform diagram
question: [DIAGRAM: waveform of a sound wave travelling left to right in air, with a distance of 33 cm labelled spanning several cycles, and a point P marked on the wave] The speed of sound in air is 330 m s$^{-1}$. What is the frequency of the sound and in which direction has the air at P been displaced from its mean position?

steps.title: From the diagram the cm span corresponds to
steps.content: From the diagram, the 33 cm span corresponds to 2.5 wavelengths, giving $\lambda=33/2.5=13.2$ cm $=0.132$ m.

steps.title: The displacement direction at P is read from
steps.content: The displacement direction at P is read from the diagram's waveform shape (to the left).

subtopic: Solving quadratics in a substituted variable
question: Find how many distinct real solutions there are to the equation $(x^2+4x+3)^2=1$.

[DIAGRAM: circuit with a 30 V dc supply, an 8.0 Ω resistor in series with a parallel combination of a 4.0 Ω heater and a resistor R connected between terminals X and Y] The power transferred in the 4.0 Ω heater is 9.0 W. What is the resistance of R?

steps.title: Voltage across the parallel section V
steps.content: Voltage across the parallel section $V_4=1.5\times4=6$ V.

steps.title: Voltage across the series resistor V giving total
steps.content: Voltage across the 8.0 Ω series resistor $=30-6=24$ V, giving total current $one=24/8=3$ A.

question: The line $x=1$ divides the circle $x^2+y^2=4$ into two segments. What is the area of the smaller segment?

steps.title: Radius chord at so distance from centre
steps.content: Radius $r=2$, chord at $x=1$, so distance from centre $d=1$.

steps.title: Using the reflex angle for the minor segment
steps.content: $\cos(\theta/2)=d/r=1/2 \Rightarrow \theta/2=\pi/3 \Rightarrow \theta=2\pi/3$. using the reflex angle for the minor segment on the far side, $\theta_{major}=2\pi-2\pi/3$; computing directly: minor segment area $=\frac12 r^2(\theta-\sin\theta)$ with $\theta=\frac{4\pi}{3}$ (angle subtended by the minor segment's arc) $=\frac12(4)\left(\frac{4\pi}{3}-\sin\frac{4\pi}{3}\right)=\frac{4\pi}{3}-\sqrt{3}$.

question: [DIAGRAM: uniform plank of length 5.0 m resting horizontally, with a pivot 1.0 m from one end, and a cable at 60° to the horizontal supporting the other end, tension 75 N] What is the weight of the plank?

steps.title: Taking moments about the pivot weight acts at
steps.content: Taking moments about the pivot: weight $W$ acts at the plank's centre, distance $2.5-1.0=1.5$ m from pivot.

steps.title: Cable acts at the far end distance m
steps.content: Cable acts at the far end, distance $5.0-1.0=4.0$ m from pivot, vertical component $=75\sin60^\circ$.

question: What is the mean of $\log_{10}27$, $\log_{10}64$, and $\log_{10}216$?

subtopic: Constant acceleration and equal time intervals
question: [DIAGRAM: straight horizontal road showing four successive oil droplets dripped from an accelerating lorry at constant time interval $t$; the distance between the first two droplets is $x$ and between the final two is $y$] A lorry accelerates along a straight, horizontal road with uniform acceleration. Oil droplets fall from the lorry onto the road at a constant time interval $t$. Which expression gives the acceleration of the lorry?

steps.title: For constant acceleration distances in successive equal time
steps.content: For constant acceleration, distances in successive equal time intervals increase by $at^2$ each interval.

steps.title: With droplets intervals the gap grows from interval
steps.content: With 4 droplets (3 intervals), the gap grows from $x$ (interval 1) to $y=x+2at^2$ (interval 3).

question: Which of the following is the largest in value? (All angles are in radians.)

steps.title: Is decreasing on so among the cosines is
steps.content: $\cos$ is decreasing on $[0,\pi/2]$, so among the cosines $\cos0.5\approx0.878$ is largest. $\sin$ is increasing, so among the sines $\sin1\approx0.841$ is largest.

steps.title: Comparing the two so is overall largest
steps.content: Comparing the two: $\cos0.5>\sin1$, so $\cos0.5$ is overall largest.

subtopic: Young modulus and elastic strain energy
question: A light, metal wire of length 2.5 m and cross-sectional area $1.8\times10^{-6}$ m$^2$ is suspended vertically. A mass of 7.2 kg is attached to the lower end of the wire. The wire extends by 0.50 mm. What is the Young modulus of the metal and how much energy is stored in the extended wire? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; assume Hooke's law holds and cross-sectional area change is negligible)

subtopic: Geometric series with sum to infinity
question: A geometric progression has first term $u_1=a$ and common ratio $r$. The sum to infinity of the geometric progression is $\frac{8}{5}$. The sum to infinity of the even-numbered terms ($u_2+u_4+u_6+\ldots$) is $\frac{3}{5}$. What is the value of $a+r$?

A child of mass 30 kg is on a sledge of mass 10 kg which is moving down a smooth slope at an instantaneous speed of 4.0 m s$^{-1}$. At this instant, the child jumps backwards off the sledge and lands stationary on the slope. What is the speed of the sledge immediately after the child jumps off?

steps.title: Total initial momentum kg m s
steps.content: Total initial momentum $=(30+10)\times4=160$ kg m s$^{-1}$.

steps.title: After jump child is stationary zero momentum so
steps.content: After jump, child is stationary (zero momentum), so all momentum belongs to the sledge: $10\times v=160 \Rightarrow v=16$ m s$^{-1}$.

question: At how many distinct points do the following two curves meet? $y=(x-4)(x^2-2x-8)$ and $y=-x^2+8x-16$

A piece of electrically conducting putty is formed into the shape of a uniform cylinder. The resistance between the ends of the cylinder is $R$. The same piece of putty is now formed into a new uniform cylinder with half the diameter of the first cylinder. What is the resistance between the ends of the new cylinder?

steps.title: Volume is conserved halving the diameter quarters the
steps.content: Volume is conserved: halving the diameter quarters the cross-sectional area ($A'=A/4$), so length quadruples ($L'=4L$) to conserve volume.

question: Evaluate $\dfrac{3}{\sqrt{27}+\sqrt{21}} + \dfrac{3}{\sqrt{24}+\sqrt{18}} + \dfrac{3}{\sqrt{21}+\sqrt{15}} + \ldots + \dfrac{3}{\sqrt9+\sqrt3}$

steps.title: Rationalise each surd term
steps.content: Each term $\dfrac{3}{\sqrt{a}+\sqrt{b}}$ rationalises to $\dfrac{\sqrt{a}-\sqrt{b}}{2}$ since $a-b=6$ throughout.

steps.title: Sum the telescoping series
steps.content: Summing the telescoping series of 7 terms ($a=27,24,\ldots,9$) leaves $\sqrt{27}+\sqrt{24}-\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{3}=3\sqrt{3}+2\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{3}=2\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{6}=\sqrt{3}(2+\sqrt{2})$.

steps.title: Divide by two for the final value

subtopic: Kinematics via integration of a time-varying acceleration
question: A car accelerates from rest in a straight line. During the first 10 s, its acceleration, $a$, in m s$^{-2}$ is given by $a=4.0-0.36t$, where $t$ is the time in seconds. What is its displacement from its original position after 10 s?

subtopic: Optimisation of a rectangle's area under a curve
question: [DIAGRAM: PQRS is a rectangle with P and Q on the x-axis, Q and R on the line x = 15, and S on the curve y = √x] What is the maximum possible area of the rectangle?

subtopic: Momentum conservation and kinetic energy split
question: Two trolleys are free to move on a smooth one-dimensional track. A light spring is compressed between the two stationary trolleys; the trolleys are released and separate. The trolleys have masses $m$ and $4m$, and the work done by the spring as it expands is $W$. Assume no work is done against friction. What is the difference in kinetic energy between the two trolleys when the spring has fully expanded?
